#REDACTED, Posted: Dec 14 2000 at 7:48 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) im a lvl 17 pally and im am having so much trouble casting my spellz, it is beginning to discourage me from the game. my casting is at lvl 91 and my wis is at 89 and my spells still fizzle all the freakin tyme, somtimes i wont even get to use the spell it fizzle so much i run out of mana.
thats just not right
QuoteReply
# Apr 18 2001 at 6:49 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
At lvl 17 your casting cant be 91 the math states otherwise.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Apr 04 2002 at 12:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766129740
21 posts
Just here playing the Devil's Advocate, if the person made level 18 and got a skill point in the skill before have a prob with bind point and chain deaths, it is very possible to have a skill of 91 in a skill at level 17. Just my 2cp :)

Dolark Dethbane
Dwarven Paladin of the 46th Stone
Zeb
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 13 2001 at 12:43 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) First off this post should be on a Paladin Board somewhere else rather than here under the Charred Breastplate thread.
RE: fizzle
QuoteReply
# Jan 11 2001 at 6:11 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Well, first of all, a skill level of 91 is not really THAT high. The same goes for your WIS; 89 is jus way too low. Remember that 200 is the mark you are shooting at for these numbers. Sure, some skills can go above that, but if you just keep the number 200 in your head, it will be easy to remember and you will find SIGNIFICANT improvement as you approach those numbers.

The WIS is the most important and changable thing. You need to up it with WIS enhancing items such as jewelry, armor and some weapons even give a WIS buff.

Your skill level will go up with time. But, in order to make sure you are up in those skills, you can do some personal training. Go to a friendly area such as inside your favorite city. (I did this in freeport) For your healing, divination and buff spells simply stand in a corner and cast away......divining, conjuring and buffing/healing yourself. You will see the skills go up just as if you were in the middle of a combat situation. Sure, there will be fizzles and mana drains....but that is the point; to suffer those mana drains in the safe privacy of your favorite city and not in the middle of a heated battle. For your damage spells you have to target a creature or, and this is the neat thing I found out by accident, a creature's corpse. So, I went outside the WFP gates and killed Orc Pawns then nuked the body until it dissapeared. I did that over and over again until my skill maxed out in those categories.

Once you get to your low 20s the skills will take care of themselves and you wont need to go do those little training sessions. But, at low levels, your skills go up so slowly that it is very nice to max them before going into battle.

I hope this helps.

Surerobbin Thenotsobrave
Druid of 41seasons
Legion of Darkmoor
Fennin Ro Server
▲ Page top

everyone is missing a very important fact..you need to put a point into your channeling skill in order to get it..and that is what keeps you from fizzling
